articles.title: A.I. Is Forcing More Belt-Tightening at Big Tech
articles.summary: 인공지능 경쟁에서 뒤처지지 않으려는 비용 부담으로 인해 대형 기술 기업들이 비용 절감에 나서고 있습니다. AI 개발에는 막대한 투자가 필요하기 때문에 기업들은 다른 분야에서 지출을 줄이고 있습니다. 이는 스타트업부터 오래된 대기업까지 업계 전반에 걸친 현상입니다.
articles.learnerContent:
## AI Is Forcing Tech Companies to Cut Costs

The artificial intelligence race is becoming increasingly expensive, and the pressure is being felt across the entire technology industry. From well-established giants to young startups, companies are being forced to **tighten their belts** — meaning they must spend more carefully and cut unnecessary costs — in order to keep up.

### The High Price of AI

Developing and running advanced AI systems requires enormous investment. Companies need to build or rent powerful data centers filled with expensive computer chips. They also need to hire highly skilled engineers and researchers, who command very high salaries in today's competitive job market.

The cost of training large AI models — the systems that power tools like chatbots and image generators — can run into hundreds of millions of dollars for a single project. Maintaining and updating these models over time adds even more to the bill.

### Who Is Affected?

The financial strain is not limited to any one type of company. Large, established tech firms such as Google, Microsoft, and Amazon have all been increasing their AI spending dramatically. While these companies have deep pockets, even they are looking for ways to offset rising AI costs by cutting spending in other areas.

Meanwhile, younger technology companies and AI startups face an even sharper challenge. Many of them are burning through cash quickly as they try to compete with much larger rivals that have far greater financial resources.

### What Kind of Cuts Are Being Made?

To manage rising AI expenses, technology companies have been making cuts in several areas. Many firms have reduced their overall headcount through layoffs, letting go of workers in divisions that are seen as less critical to their AI strategies. Some companies have also scaled back on office space, marketing budgets, and non-AI research projects.

The trend reflects a broader shift in priorities across the industry. Investment and talent are increasingly being concentrated in AI, while other parts of the business receive less attention and fewer resources.

### A High-Stakes Competition

The reason companies feel they cannot simply slow down their AI spending is the fear of falling behind. AI is widely seen as the most important technology of the coming decade, with the potential to transform industries ranging from healthcare and finance to transportation and entertainment.

Companies that fail to keep pace risk losing customers, talent, and market share to faster-moving competitors. This creates a cycle where everyone feels compelled to spend more, even as costs continue to climb.

### The Bigger Picture

The AI spending race raises important questions about sustainability. Can companies continue to invest at this pace without damaging their financial health? And what happens to the workers and projects that are cut in order to fund AI ambitions?

For now, the pressure to compete in AI shows no sign of easing, and cost-cutting looks set to remain a defining feature of the tech industry for the foreseeable future.

keyVocab.example: After losing several clients, the agency had to tighten its belt and reduce staff.
The company planted trees to offset the carbon emissions from its factories.
The firm reduced its headcount by 15% through a series of layoffs.
Due to budget constraints, the project team decided to scale back their original plans.
Investors are questioning the sustainability of the company's rapid growth.

articles.title: Ignoring the War Has Been Working for Long-Term Investors
articles.summary: 미국 주식 시장은 이번 달 빠르게 회복되었지만, 이 반등은 불안정할 수 있습니다. 전문가들은 시장의 불안한 변동을 견디며 장기적으로 투자를 유지하는 전략이 효과적이었다고 말합니다. 전쟁과 같은 지정학적 위기 속에서도 장기 투자자들은 꾸준히 좋은 성과를 거두어 왔습니다.
articles.learnerContent:
## Ignoring the War Has Been Working for Long-Term Investors

The U.S. stock market has had a rocky few months. Prices have gone up and down sharply, and many investors have felt nervous. However, for people who stayed calm and kept their money invested, the results have been surprisingly good.

### A Quick Rebound

In April, the U.S. stock market recovered quickly after a sharp drop. This means that stock prices fell suddenly, but then rose again in a short period of time. This kind of fast recovery is sometimes called a "rebound."

However, financial experts warn that this rebound may be **fragile**. In other words, it might not last long. The market could fall again if the situation changes. Investors should not assume that the danger is completely over.

### What Is Driving the Uncertainty?

One major reason for market uncertainty is the ongoing war in Ukraine and other global conflicts. When wars happen, they can affect energy prices, supply chains, and the overall global economy. This makes investors worried, and sometimes they sell their stocks out of fear.

Geopolitical events — things like wars, elections, and international disputes — have always caused short-term trouble in financial markets. History shows, however, that these events rarely change the long-term direction of the market.

### The Strategy That Has Been Working

So what have successful investors been doing? Many of them have simply chosen to **ride out** the difficult periods. This means they did not panic and sell their stocks when prices dropped. Instead, they held onto their investments and waited for the market to recover.

This approach is sometimes called a "buy and hold" strategy. Instead of trying to guess when to buy and when to sell, long-term investors keep their money in the market through both good times and bad times.

Historically, this strategy has produced strong results. The U.S. stock market has always recovered from wars, recessions, and other crises — even if it sometimes takes months or years. Investors who stayed in the market during these difficult periods were usually rewarded in the end.

### Why Do People Panic?

Even though history supports the "ride it out" approach, many investors still panic when markets fall. This is because losing money — even temporarily — feels very painful. Psychologists call this "loss aversion." People feel the pain of losing $100 much more strongly than they feel the pleasure of gaining $100.

This emotional reaction can lead investors to make poor decisions. They sell their stocks when prices are low, and then they miss the recovery when prices rise again.

### What Should Investors Remember?

Financial advisors often give the following advice to investors during uncertain times:

- **Stay calm.** Do not make big decisions based on short-term fear.
- **Think long-term.** If you do not need your money for 10 or 20 years, short-term drops are less important.
- **Diversify.** Spread your money across different types of investments to reduce risk.
- **Avoid checking prices too often.** Looking at your portfolio every day can increase anxiety and lead to bad decisions.

### The Bottom Line

The U.S. stock market's recent rebound may be fragile, and more uncertainty could be ahead. But for investors with a long time horizon, the strategy of staying invested and ignoring short-term noise has historically been a smart one. As the saying goes, "time in the market beats timing the market."

keyVocab.example: After the sharp drop, the stock market had a strong rebound within just a few weeks.
The economic recovery looks promising, but experts say it is still fragile and could reverse.
Many experienced investors chose to ride out the market crash instead of selling their stocks.
Because of loss aversion, people often make emotional decisions when their investments lose value.
It is wise to diversify your portfolio by investing in stocks, bonds, and real estate.

articles.title: Mamdani Expected to Reject Bill Involving Police at School Protests
articles.summary: 뉴욕시장 마므다니가 학교 근처 시위 관련 경찰 계획 공개를 의무화하는 법안에 거부권을 행사할 것으로 예상됩니다. 이는 시장으로서 첫 번째 거부권 행사가 될 것입니다. 해당 법안은 경찰이 교육 시설 근처의 시위대를 어떻게 관리할지 계획을 공개하도록 강제하는 내용을 담고 있습니다.
articles.learnerContent:
## New York Mayor Expected to Veto Police Protest Bill

New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani is expected to reject a new bill that relates to how police handle protesters near schools. If he does, this will be his first veto since becoming mayor.

**What is a veto?**

A veto is when a leader, such as a mayor or president, officially refuses to approve a law that has been passed by a council or parliament. When a mayor vetoes a bill, it does not become law — at least not automatically.

**What does the bill say?**

The bill would require the New York City Police Department (NYPD) to publicly release its plans for managing protesters who gather near schools and other educational facilities. Supporters of the bill believe that people have a right to know how the police intend to respond to protests, especially near places where children are present.

In recent years, protests near schools have become more common in New York City. Some demonstrations have been related to political issues, such as conflicts overseas or education policy. These events have sometimes led to concerns about how police interact with both students and protesters.

**Why might the mayor reject it?**

Mayor Mamdani has not given a full public explanation yet, but city officials close to the mayor suggest he has concerns about the bill. Some officials worry that forcing the police to reveal their crowd management strategies in advance could make it harder for officers to do their jobs safely and effectively. Others argue that too much public detail about police plans could create security risks.

On the other hand, civil liberties groups and some city council members support the bill. They argue that transparency — meaning openness about what the government does — is important in a democratic city. They believe residents should be able to see how the police plan to handle situations near schools.

**What happens next?**

If Mayor Mamdani vetoes the bill, the New York City Council could still try to override his decision. To do that, they would need a two-thirds majority vote — meaning at least two out of every three council members would have to vote in favor of the bill. If they succeed, the bill would become law even without the mayor's approval.

This situation highlights a common tension in city government between police operational freedom and the public's right to information. How New York City handles this debate may influence how other large cities approach similar issues in the future.

keyVocab.example: The mayor used his veto to stop the new law from being passed.
The new rule would compel all schools to report safety incidents.
Citizens demanded transparency about how their tax money was being spent.
The council voted to override the mayor's veto and pass the law anyway.
A large demonstration took place outside the city hall to protest the new policy.

articles.title: I don’t think Gwyneth Paltrow knows what a peptide is
articles.summary: 펩타이드는 요즘 건강 및 웰니스 세계에서 매우 유행하는 성분이 되었습니다. 소셜 미디어, 광고, 정치계에서도 펩타이드가 언급되고 있지만, 많은 사람들이 펩타이드가 정확히 무엇인지 제대로 이해하지 못하는 것처럼 보입니다.
articles.learnerContent:
## Peptides Are Everywhere — But Does Anyone Actually Know What They Are?

If you spend any time online or commuting through a big city these days, you have probably noticed one word appearing over and over again: **peptide**. It is showing up in beauty products, health supplements, fitness communities, political speeches, and subway advertisements. But what exactly is a peptide, and why is everyone suddenly talking about them?

**What Is a Peptide?**

A peptide is a short chain of **amino acids** — the building blocks that make up proteins in the human body. Because peptides are smaller than full proteins, they can be absorbed more easily by the body or skin. Scientists have studied certain peptides for their potential roles in muscle repair, skin health, hormone regulation, and more.

In medicine, some peptides are used in legitimate treatments. For example, **GLP-1 receptor agonists** — a type of peptide-based drug — are at the center of the current weight-loss medication boom, with drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy generating enormous public interest.

**Peptides in Pop Culture**

Tech columnist Victoria Song of *The Verge* writes that peptides have become almost impossible to avoid. On social media, she describes being flooded with videos of fitness influencers injecting what they call the "Wolverine stack" — a combination of peptides supposedly inspired by the superhero's legendary healing ability. These products are often sourced from unregulated suppliers, raising serious safety concerns.

On the New York City subway, tennis legend Serena Williams appears in advertisements for **Ro**, a telehealth company promoting easier access to GLP-1 medications. Meanwhile, in Silicon Valley — home to the tech industry's obsession with **biohacking** and human optimization — peptide-themed social events have reportedly become fashionable among the wealthy and health-conscious.

**Even Politicians Are Talking About Peptides**

The trend has reached Washington, D.C. as well. Robert F. Kennedy Jr. (RFK Jr.), who serves in a health-related government role, has publicly stated that he supports peptides and wants to expand public access to them. His comments signal that peptides are no longer just a niche wellness topic — they have entered mainstream political conversation.

**The Problem: Hype Without Understanding**

Despite all this attention, columnist Victoria Song raises an important point: many of the people promoting peptides — including celebrities — may not fully understand what they are talking about. She humorously suggests that actress and wellness entrepreneur Gwyneth Paltrow has certainly *heard* of peptides, but probably cannot explain what they actually do at a biological level.

This is a common pattern in the wellness industry. A scientific-sounding ingredient gains popularity, celebrities and influencers begin endorsing it, and consumers start buying products without fully understanding the science — or the potential risks.

**Should You Be Careful?**

Not all peptide products are dangerous, and some have genuine scientific support. However, experts warn that many products sold online are **unregulated**, meaning their purity and safety have not been independently verified. Injecting substances from unknown sources carries real medical risks.

As with any health trend, the advice is straightforward: before trying a new supplement or treatment, consult a qualified doctor rather than relying on social media influencers or celebrity endorsements.

keyVocab.meaning: 아미노산의 짧은 사슬로, 단백질의 구성 요소이며 건강 및 미용 제품에 많이 사용됨
keyVocab.example: Some skincare creams contain peptides that are claimed to help reduce wrinkles.

keyVocab.meaning: 신체 기능을 향상시키기 위해 과학적 방법이나 기술을 실험적으로 적용하는 행위
keyVocab.example: Silicon Valley entrepreneurs interested in biohacking often experiment with special diets and supplements.

keyVocab.meaning: 정부나 공식 기관의 규제나 검사를 받지 않은
keyVocab.example: Buying medicine from unregulated online sources can be very dangerous.

keyVocab.meaning: 유명인이나 전문가가 특정 제품이나 아이디어를 공개적으로 지지하거나 추천하는 것
keyVocab.example: The sports drink saw a huge rise in sales after a celebrity endorsement.

keyVocab.meaning: 인터넷이나 전화를 통해 원격으로 의료 서비스를 제공하는 방식
keyVocab.example: Through a telehealth app, she was able to speak with a doctor without leaving her home.

articles.title: ‘Scooby-Doo: Origins’ First Look: Netflix Series Reveals Mystery Inc. Crew, Including Mckenna Grace as Daphne
articles.summary: 넷플릭스가 실사 시리즈 '스쿠비-두: 오리진스'의 첫 번째 이미지를 공개했습니다. 매켄나 그레이스가 대프니 역을 맡으며 미스터리 주식회사 팀이 소개되었습니다. 단, 주인공 스쿠비-두는 아직 모습을 드러내지 않았습니다.
articles.learnerContent:
## Netflix Reveals First Look at 'Scooby-Doo: Origins'

Netflix has shared the first official images from its upcoming live-action series **"Scooby-Doo: Origins."** The photos introduce the famous Mystery Inc. gang, but there is one big surprise — Scooby-Doo himself is not in the pictures yet.

### Meet the New Mystery Inc. Gang

Fans of the classic cartoon will recognize the group of young mystery-solvers. The cast includes **Mckenna Grace**, who plays Daphne. Grace is a well-known young actress who has appeared in movies such as *Ghostbusters: Afterlife* and *Gifted*. The rest of the Mystery Inc. crew is also being introduced for the first time through these early images.

The show is currently being filmed in **Atlanta, Georgia**. Production has already begun, which means the series is moving forward quickly.

### Where Is Scooby-Doo?

The most talked-about detail from the first look is that Scooby-Doo — the lovable, cowardly, and snack-loving Great Dane dog — does not appear in the photos. This is likely because Scooby will be a **CGI (computer-generated) character**, meaning he will be created digitally and added to the footage later in post-production. This is a common technique used in modern TV shows and movies when animals or fantasy creatures need to look realistic.

### What Is 'Scooby-Doo: Origins' About?

As the title suggests, "Scooby-Doo: Origins" will tell the **origin story** of the Mystery Inc. gang. An origin story explains how characters first met and how they began their adventures together. For long-time Scooby-Doo fans, this is an exciting chance to see a fresh and modern version of a story they already love.

The original *Scooby-Doo* cartoon first appeared on American television in **1969**. Since then, it has become one of the most recognized and beloved animated franchises in the world. The story always follows the same basic idea: a group of teenagers and their talking dog travel together and solve spooky mysteries, only to find that the "monsters" are usually just people in disguise.

### Why This Matters

This live-action adaptation is part of a growing trend of turning classic animated shows into real-life productions. Netflix has had success with similar projects in the past. By hiring recognizable young talent like Mckenna Grace and filming in a major production city like Atlanta, the streaming giant is clearly investing heavily in this show.

Fans around the world are already excited and curious about how Scooby-Doo will look as a CGI character alongside real human actors. More details, including a release date, are expected to be announced in the coming months.

For now, the first look gives audiences a promising glimpse at a brand-new chapter for one of television's most classic and timeless franchises.

keyVocab.meaning: 실사 (애니메이션이나 CGI가 아닌 실제 배우가 출연하는)
keyVocab.example: The live-action version of the cartoon was filmed with real actors in a real city.
The dragon in the movie was a CGI character created entirely on a computer.

keyVocab.meaning: 기원 이야기, 캐릭터가 처음 어떻게 시작했는지 설명하는 이야기
keyVocab.example: The new series is an origin story that shows how the heroes first became a team.

keyVocab.meaning: 프랜차이즈, 동일한 캐릭터나 세계관을 바탕으로 한 일련의 작품들
keyVocab.example: The superhero franchise has produced more than ten movies over the past twenty years.

keyVocab.meaning: 후반 작업 (촬영 후 편집, 음향, 시각 효과 등을 추가하는 단계)
keyVocab.example: The special effects were added during post-production, after all the filming was finished.
